Off Calendar Jun-28-2021 Continued To Aug-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PTR-15-299001 The Sara R. Julian 2007 Trust and
PTR-19-302554 The Sara R. Julian Special Needs Trsut.

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg appeared by zoom for Gene Marchi (Trustee of the 2007 Trust) and for David Levin (Trustee of the Special Needs Trust).

The Court waives the filing of an information-only inventory and appraisal by Mr. Levin and noted that there are missing pages of the 2007 trust. Ms. Friedeberg stated that Mr. Marchi is not intending to terminate the trust, that this was intended to be a final accounting, that the balance of the 2007 trust is now at a zero and that there is no reason why it can't be terminated. The Court invites Ms. Friedeberg to file an amendment to the petition to terminate the 2007 Trust. The Third and Final Account and Report of Successor Trustee; Petition for Its Settlement; for Approval of Trustee's and Attorneys' Fees and Costs Advanced; for Distribution of Property on Hand; for Exoneration of Bond; and for Discharge as Trustee, and the First Account and Report of Trustee; Petition for Its Settlement; for Approval of Trustee's and Attorneys' Fees and Costs Advanced; for Allowance of Payments to Trust Beneficiary; for Authorization of Allowance Payments to Trust Beneficiary; for Authorization of Periodic Payments to Trustee; and for Reduction of Bond are continued to August 4, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

After brief discussion, the Accounting Petition is continued to June 28, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-10-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Gene Marchi.

The Court stated before it today is the Second Account and Report of Successor Trustee; Petition for its Settlement; for Approval of Trustee's and Attorneys' Fees and Costs Advanced; and for Reduction of Bond. The Court inquired if Ms. Friedeberg received the examiner's notes. Ms. Friedeberg confirmed receipt of the notes. The Court stated that it would approve the reduction of bond, allow the investments, allow the Trustee to invest in the same manner for the next account period, and approve the attorney's fees. The Court addressed the accounting and the Trustee's fees. Ms. Friedeberg addressed the overpayment stating that the overpayment was reimbursed by the Trustee, that the Trustee made errors in entries, that there were entries in the Trust that should have been in the conservatorship, that the Trustee apologizes for those errors, that they have provided detailed time logs & notes, and that they weren't clear on what else the Court needed. Ms. Friedeberg also addressed the request for Trustee's fees at one percent of the fair market value stating that there is an extreme level of liability that a Trustee assumes, that the Trustee takes on the responsibility of that liabilty, that there are things that come up that the Trustee needs to be prepared to defend, that the requested Trustee's fees aren't purely for time spent, that the fee request includes the liability that the Trustee takes on as well, and that if a Trustee were to be sued that part of the one percent would be to cover that cost. At the conclusion of Ms. Friedeberg's argument, the Court took the Second Account and Report of Successor Trustee; Petition for its Settlement; for Approval of Trustee's and Attorneys' Fees and Costs Advanced; and for Reduction of Bond under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-13-296787 Conservatorship of Sara R. Julian and
PTR-15-299001 The Sara R. Julian Trust.

Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Gene Marchi.
Marta Eisenberg Court appointed Counsel for Conservatee.

The Court questioned why payment with regard to the schooling shouldn't made directly to the school rather than to the Father. Ms. Friedeberg stated that it can be done, that her Mother is no longer doing the accountings, that this accounting was done by Mr. Marchi, that the $105.00 fee payment that was made was left over from the prior accounting, and spoke of the Son's medical and emotional condition. Ms. Eisenberg stated she met with her Client, that her Client echoed her wish to terminate the conservatorship and that it should not have been put in place, and that she explained to her Client that she has not shown enough progress or evidence that is needed for termination. The First Account and Report of Conservator, etc. and the First Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. are taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Continued To Mar-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (252-256 Hartford Street, San Francisco, Ca 94114)
Off Calendar Feb-14-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Bruce A. Feder, for petitioner Gene Marchi.

The Court noted that the petition was for approval of sale of the trust's 30% interest in the real property located at 252-256 Hartford Street, San Francisco, but that 100% of the property was being sold in the sale auction. The opening bid for the property was $3,215,000.00, subject to the terms of the report of sale filed January 9, 2017. The Court found this to be a qualifying bid. The Court noted the sale at this price would require an additional bond in the sum of $377,000.00 There were no overbidders for the property. The sale was confirmed at $3,215,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $377,000.00 is required.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy A. Jaime. (Not reported)
